products.name: Instrument Master
products.description: System of record for all tradable financial instruments including equities, fixed income, derivatives, and foreign exchange products. Contains reference data such as identifiers, classifications, lifecycle dates, and issuer information.
products.schema_sql:
CREATE TABLE instrument_master (
  instrument_id VARCHAR(20) PRIMARY KEY,
  isin_code VARCHAR(12),
  cusip_code VARCHAR(9),
  sedol_code VARCHAR(7),
  instrument_name VARCHAR(200),
  instrument_type_code VARCHAR(10),
  asset_class_code VARCHAR(10),
  issue_date DATE,
  maturity_date DATE,
  expiry_date DATE,
  issuer_party_id VARCHAR(20),
  currency_code VARCHAR(3),
  country_of_issue VARCHAR(3),
  exchange_code VARCHAR(10),
  notional_amount DECIMAL(18,2),
  coupon_rate DECIMAL(8,4),
  coupon_frequency VARCHAR(10),
  rating_sp VARCHAR(10),
  rating_moodys VARCHAR(10),
  status_code VARCHAR(5),
  created_date TIMESTAMP,
  last_updated_date TIMESTAMP
);
products.schema_graphql:
type Instrument {
  instrumentId: ID!
  isinCode: String
  cusipCode: String
  sedolCode: String
  instrumentName: String!
  instrumentTypeCode: String!
  assetClassCode: String!
  issueDate: Date
  maturityDate: Date
  expiryDate: Date
  issuerPartyId: String
  currencyCode: String!
  countryOfIssue: String
  exchangeCode: String
  notionalAmount: Float
  couponRate: Float
  couponFrequency: String
  ratingSp: String
  ratingMoodys: String
  statusCode: String!
  createdDate: DateTime
  lastUpdatedDate: DateTime
}

type Query {
  instrument(instrumentId: ID!): Instrument
  instruments(filter: InstrumentFilter, limit: Int, offset: Int): [Instrument!]!
}

input InstrumentFilter {
  instrumentTypeCode: String
  assetClassCode: String
  currencyCode: String
  statusCode: String
  issuerPartyId: String
  exchangeCode: String
}

attributes.description: Unique identifier for the financial instrument
International Securities Identification Number - a 12-character alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies a security
Committee on Uniform Securities Identification Procedures number - 9-character identifier for North American securities
Stock Exchange Daily Official List code - 7-character identifier used in the UK and Ireland
Full legal name of the financial instrument
Classification code indicating the type of instrument

value_dictionary.SWAP: Interest rate or credit default swap

attributes.description: Broad classification of the instrument's asset class
Date when the instrument was originally issued
Date when the instrument matures or expires
Date when the instrument record expires in the system
Identifier of the party that issued the instrument, references party_master.party_id
ISO 4217 currency code for the instrument's denomination
ISO 3166 country code where the instrument was issued
Code of the primary exchange where the instrument is listed
Face value or notional principal amount of the instrument
Annual coupon rate for fixed income instruments, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0525 = 5.25%)
How often coupon payments are made
Standard & Poor's credit rating for the instrument
Moody's credit rating for the instrument
Current lifecycle status of the instrument

value_dictionary.A: Active - instrument is live and tradable
value_dictionary.I: Inactive - instrument is no longer tradable
value_dictionary.M: Matured - instrument has reached maturity date

business_rules.description: An instrument is considered active when its status is Active and it has not passed its expiry date
business_rules.sql_predicate: status_code = 'A' AND (expiry_date IS NULL OR expiry_date > CURRENT_DATE())

business_rules.description: A bond that has reached its maturity date
A bond rated below investment grade (BB+ or lower by S&P, Ba1 or lower by Moody's)
A bond rated BBB- or higher by S&P (Baa3 or higher by Moody's)
All equity-class instruments including common stocks, preferred shares, and ETFs

query_examples.question: Show me all active bonds maturing in 2025
query_examples.query:
SELECT instrument_id, instrument_name, isin_code, maturity_date, coupon_rate, currency_code
FROM instrument_master
WHERE status_code = 'A'
  AND (expiry_date IS NULL OR expiry_date > CURRENT_DATE())
  AND instrument_type_code = 'BOND'
  AND maturity_date BETWEEN '2025-01-01' AND '2025-12-31'
ORDER BY maturity_date;

query_examples.question: What are the high yield bonds issued by a specific party?
query_examples.query:
SELECT i.instrument_id, i.instrument_name, i.isin_code, i.rating_sp, i.coupon_rate, i.maturity_date
FROM instrument_master i
WHERE i.status_code = 'A'
  AND i.instrument_type_code = 'BOND'
  AND i.rating_sp IN ('BB+','BB','BB-','B+','B','B-','CCC+','CCC','CCC-','CC','C','D')
  AND i.issuer_party_id = :party_id
ORDER BY i.rating_sp, i.maturity_date;

query_examples.question: List all equities traded on NYSE
query_examples.query:
SELECT instrument_id, instrument_name, isin_code, currency_code
FROM instrument_master
WHERE status_code = 'A'
  AND (expiry_date IS NULL OR expiry_date > CURRENT_DATE())
  AND asset_class_code = 'EQ'
  AND exchange_code = 'NYSE'
ORDER BY instrument_name;

query_examples.question: Find the ISIN for a bond called Treasury 5.25 2030
query_examples.query:
SELECT instrument_id, isin_code, cusip_code, instrument_name, maturity_date, coupon_rate
FROM instrument_master
WHERE instrument_name ILIKE '%Treasury%5.25%2030%'
  AND instrument_type_code = 'BOND';

query_examples.question: Get instrument details by ISIN

products.name: Market Data
products.description: End-of-day and intraday market data including prices, yields, spreads, and trading volumes for all asset classes. Sourced from exchanges, data vendors (Bloomberg, Reuters), and internal marks.
products.schema_sql:
CREATE TABLE market_data (
  instrument_id VARCHAR(20),
  price_date DATE,
  bid_price DECIMAL(18,6),
  ask_price DECIMAL(18,6),
  mid_price DECIMAL(18,6),
  close_price DECIMAL(18,6),
  open_price DECIMAL(18,6),
  high_price DECIMAL(18,6),
  low_price DECIMAL(18,6),
  volume BIGINT,
  vwap DECIMAL(18,6),
  yield_to_maturity DECIMAL(8,4),
  spread_bps DECIMAL(8,2),
  price_currency VARCHAR(3),
  price_source VARCHAR(20),
  as_of_timestamp TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY (instrument_id, price_date, price_source)
);

attributes.description: Identifier of the instrument this price data belongs to, references instrument_master.instrument_id
Business date for this price record
Best bid price at close
Best ask/offer price at close
Midpoint between bid and ask price
Official closing price for the business day
Opening price for the business day
Highest traded price during the business day
Lowest traded price during the business day
Total number of shares or contracts traded during the business day
Volume-weighted average price for the business day
Yield to maturity for fixed income instruments
Credit spread over benchmark in basis points
Currency in which the price is quoted
Source of the price data

business_rules.description: The most recent available price for an instrument — use MAX(price_date) or order by price_date DESC LIMIT 1
business_rules.sql_predicate: price_date = (SELECT MAX(price_date) FROM market_data md2 WHERE md2.instrument_id = market_data.instrument_id)

business_rules.description: When users ask for 'the price', they typically mean the close_price field
When multiple sources exist, prefer BLOOMBERG, then REUTERS, then EXCHANGE
When a user asks for price 'as of' a date, use price_date = that date. If not available, use the most recent prior date.
business_rules.sql_predicate: price_date <= :as_of_date ORDER BY price_date DESC LIMIT 1

query_examples.question: What is the latest closing price for instrument X?
query_examples.query:
SELECT instrument_id, price_date, close_price, price_currency, price_source
FROM market_data
WHERE instrument_id = :instrument_id
  AND close_price IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY price_date DESC
LIMIT 1;

query_examples.question: Show me the price history for the last 30 days
query_examples.query:
SELECT instrument_id, price_date, open_price, high_price, low_price, close_price, volume
FROM market_data
WHERE instrument_id = :instrument_id
  AND price_date >= DATEADD(day, -30, CURRENT_DATE())
  AND price_source = 'BLOOMBERG'
ORDER BY price_date;

query_examples.question: Which instruments had the highest trading volume yesterday?
query_examples.query:
SELECT m.instrument_id, m.close_price, m.volume, m.price_currency
FROM market_data m
WHERE m.price_date = DATEADD(day, -1, CURRENT_DATE())
  AND m.price_source = 'BLOOMBERG'
ORDER BY m.volume DESC
LIMIT 20;

query_examples.question: What is the yield to maturity for bond ABC?
query_examples.query:
SELECT instrument_id, price_date, yield_to_maturity, spread_bps, close_price
FROM market_data
WHERE instrument_id = :instrument_id
  AND yield_to_maturity IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY price_date DESC
LIMIT 1;

query_examples.question: Show me the bid-ask spread for instrument X over the past week
query_examples.query:
SELECT instrument_id, price_date, bid_price, ask_price, (ask_price - bid_price) AS bid_ask_spread
FROM market_data
WHERE instrument_id = :instrument_id
  AND price_date >= DATEADD(day, -7, CURRENT_DATE())
  AND price_source = 'BLOOMBERG'
ORDER BY price_date;

products.name: Party Master
products.description: System of record for all legal entities, counterparties, issuers, custodians, and internal business units. Contains party identification, classification, geographic information, and regulatory identifiers like LEI codes.
products.schema_sql:
CREATE TABLE party_master (
  party_id VARCHAR(20) PRIMARY KEY,
  legal_name VARCHAR(300),
  short_name VARCHAR(100),
  party_type_code VARCHAR(10),
  party_role_code VARCHAR(10),
  lei_code VARCHAR(20),
  tax_id VARCHAR(30),
  country_code VARCHAR(3),
  region_code VARCHAR(10),
  city VARCHAR(100),
  industry_code VARCHAR(10),
  parent_party_id VARCHAR(20),
  ultimate_parent_id VARCHAR(20),
  status_code VARCHAR(5),
  onboarding_date DATE,
  last_kyc_date DATE,
  created_date TIMESTAMP,
  last_updated_date TIMESTAMP
);
products.schema_graphql:
type Party {
  partyId: ID!
  legalName: String!
  shortName: String
  partyTypeCode: String!
  partyRoleCode: String
  leiCode: String
  taxId: String
  countryCode: String
  regionCode: String
  city: String
  industryCode: String
  parentPartyId: String
  ultimateParentId: String
  statusCode: String!
  onboardingDate: Date
  lastKycDate: Date
}

type Query {
  party(partyId: ID!): Party
  parties(filter: PartyFilter, limit: Int, offset: Int): [Party!]!
}

input PartyFilter {
  partyTypeCode: String
  partyRoleCode: String
  countryCode: String
  regionCode: String
  statusCode: String
}

attributes.description: Unique identifier for the party/legal entity
Full legal name of the entity
Abbreviated or commonly used name
Classification of the party entity type
The role this party plays in business relationships
Legal Entity Identifier - 20-character global identifier for legal entities in financial transactions
ISO 3166 country code of the party's domicile
Geographic region classification
Industry sector classification of the party
Immediate parent entity in the corporate hierarchy
Top-level parent entity in the corporate hierarchy
Current status of the party record
Date when the party was onboarded as a counterparty
Date of the most recent Know Your Customer review

business_rules.description: A party is considered active when status is Active
Excludes internal business units from party queries unless specifically requested
A party whose KYC review is overdue (last review more than 12 months ago)
Parties in the Asia Pacific region

query_examples.question: List all active counterparties in APAC region
query_examples.query:
SELECT party_id, legal_name, short_name, country_code, party_type_code, industry_code
FROM party_master
WHERE status_code = 'A'
  AND party_type_code != 'INTRL'
  AND region_code = 'APAC'
ORDER BY legal_name;

query_examples.question: Find the LEI code for Goldman Sachs
query_examples.query:
SELECT party_id, legal_name, lei_code, country_code
FROM party_master
WHERE legal_name ILIKE '%Goldman Sachs%'
  OR short_name ILIKE '%Goldman%';

query_examples.question: Which counterparties have overdue KYC reviews?
query_examples.query:
SELECT party_id, legal_name, last_kyc_date, DATEDIFF(month, last_kyc_date, CURRENT_DATE()) AS months_since_kyc
FROM party_master
WHERE status_code = 'A'
  AND party_type_code != 'INTRL'
  AND last_kyc_date < DATEADD(month, -12, CURRENT_DATE())
ORDER BY last_kyc_date;

query_examples.question: Show me the corporate hierarchy for a given party
query_examples.query:
SELECT p.party_id, p.legal_name, p.parent_party_id,
  pp.legal_name AS parent_name,
  p.ultimate_parent_id,
  up.legal_name AS ultimate_parent_name
FROM party_master p
LEFT JOIN party_master pp ON p.parent_party_id = pp.party_id
LEFT JOIN party_master up ON p.ultimate_parent_id = up.party_id
WHERE p.party_id = :party_id;

query_examples.question: Get details for a counterparty

products.name: Account Master
products.description: System of record for all trading, custody, and settlement accounts. Contains account identification, classification, ownership, and custodian information linking accounts to parties and business units.
products.schema_sql:
CREATE TABLE account_master (
  account_id VARCHAR(20) PRIMARY KEY,
  account_name VARCHAR(200),
  account_type_code VARCHAR(10),
  account_category VARCHAR(20),
  owning_party_id VARCHAR(20),
  custodian_party_id VARCHAR(20),
  prime_broker_id VARCHAR(20),
  base_currency VARCHAR(3),
  booking_entity VARCHAR(20),
  desk_code VARCHAR(10),
  strategy_code VARCHAR(20),
  status_code VARCHAR(5),
  open_date DATE,
  close_date DATE,
  created_date TIMESTAMP,
  last_updated_date TIMESTAMP
);

attributes.description: Unique identifier for the account
Descriptive name of the account
Classification of the account type
Broader categorization of the account
The party that owns this account, references party_master.party_id
The custodian bank holding assets in this account, references party_master.party_id
Prime broker associated with this account, references party_master.party_id
Base reporting currency for the account
Internal legal entity that books trades in this account
Trading desk associated with this account
Investment or trading strategy classification
Current status of the account

value_dictionary.A: Active - open and available for trading

attributes.description: Date when the account was opened
Date when the account was closed, NULL if still open

business_rules.description: An account is active when status is Active and close_date is NULL
Accounts held at external custodians
business_rules.sql_predicate: account_type_code = 'CUSTODY' AND custodian_party_id IS NOT NULL

business_rules.description: House/proprietary trading accounts (not client accounts)
Accounts belonging to external clients

query_examples.question: Show me all active trading accounts for the equity desk
query_examples.query:
SELECT account_id, account_name, account_type_code, base_currency, strategy_code
FROM account_master
WHERE status_code = 'A'
  AND close_date IS NULL
  AND account_type_code = 'TRADING'
  AND desk_code IN ('EQ_CASH', 'EQ_DERIV')
ORDER BY account_name;

query_examples.question: Which accounts are held at custodian X?
query_examples.query:
SELECT a.account_id, a.account_name, a.account_type_code, a.base_currency,
  p.legal_name AS custodian_name
FROM account_master a
JOIN party_master p ON a.custodian_party_id = p.party_id
WHERE a.status_code = 'A'
  AND a.close_date IS NULL
  AND a.custodian_party_id = :custodian_id
ORDER BY a.account_name;

query_examples.question: List all client accounts opened this year
query_examples.query:
SELECT account_id, account_name, owning_party_id, base_currency, open_date
FROM account_master
WHERE account_category = 'AGENCY'
  AND account_type_code = 'CLIENT'
  AND open_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE())
ORDER BY open_date DESC;

query_examples.question: How many accounts does each desk have?
query_examples.query:
SELECT desk_code, COUNT(*) AS account_count
FROM account_master
WHERE status_code = 'A' AND close_date IS NULL
GROUP BY desk_code
ORDER BY account_count DESC;

products.name: Trade Ledger
products.description: Record of all executed trades including equities, fixed income, derivatives, and FX. Contains trade economics, counterparty information, settlement details, and lifecycle events from execution through settlement.
products.schema_sql:
CREATE TABLE trade_ledger (
  trade_id VARCHAR(30) PRIMARY KEY,
  instrument_id VARCHAR(20),
  account_id VARCHAR(20),
  counterparty_id VARCHAR(20),
  trade_date DATE,
  settlement_date DATE,
  value_date DATE,
  trade_type VARCHAR(10),
  buy_sell_indicator VARCHAR(4),
  quantity DECIMAL(18,4),
  price DECIMAL(18,6),
  gross_amount DECIMAL(18,2),
  net_amount DECIMAL(18,2),
  commission DECIMAL(18,2),
  fees DECIMAL(18,2),
  currency_code VARCHAR(3),
  settlement_currency VARCHAR(3),
  execution_venue VARCHAR(20),
  broker_id VARCHAR(20),
  trader_id VARCHAR(20),
  status_code VARCHAR(10),
  booking_date TIMESTAMP,
  created_date TIMESTAMP,
  last_updated_date TIMESTAMP
);

attributes.description: Unique identifier for the trade
Identifier of the traded instrument, references instrument_master.instrument_id
Account in which the trade is booked, references account_master.account_id
The other party in the trade, references party_master.party_id
Date when the trade was executed
Date when the trade settles (delivery vs payment)
Type of trade transaction
Whether the trade is a buy or sell from our perspective
Number of units/shares/contracts traded
Execution price per unit
Total trade amount before fees (quantity * price)
Total trade amount after fees and commissions
Broker commission charged on the trade
Trade currency
Where the trade was executed
Executing broker, references party_master.party_id
Identifier of the trader who executed the trade
Current lifecycle status of the trade

business_rules.description: Only include trades that are confirmed or settled; exclude cancelled, rejected, and pending trades
Trades that have fully settled
Trades that are confirmed but not yet settled
Trades that failed to settle on the intended settlement date
business_rules.sql_predicate: status_code = 'FAIL' OR (status_code IN ('CONF', 'NEW') AND settlement_date < CURRENT_DATE())

business_rules.description: Trades where we are the buyer
Trades where we are the seller

query_examples.question: Show me all trades executed today
query_examples.query:
SELECT trade_id, instrument_id, buy_sell_indicator, quantity, price, gross_amount, currency_code, counterparty_id, status_code
FROM trade_ledger
WHERE trade_date = CURRENT_DATE()
  AND status_code NOT IN ('CXL', 'REJ')
ORDER BY booking_date DESC;

query_examples.question: What are the unsettled trades for account X?
query_examples.query:
SELECT t.trade_id, t.instrument_id, t.trade_date, t.settlement_date, t.buy_sell_indicator,
  t.quantity, t.price, t.net_amount, t.currency_code, t.status_code
FROM trade_ledger t
WHERE t.account_id = :account_id
  AND t.status_code IN ('CONF', 'NEW')
  AND t.settlement_date >= CURRENT_DATE()
ORDER BY t.settlement_date;

query_examples.question: Total buy volume by instrument this month
query_examples.query:
SELECT instrument_id, SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity, SUM(gross_amount) AS total_value, COUNT(*) AS trade_count
FROM trade_ledger
WHERE trade_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE())
  AND buy_sell_indicator = 'BUY'
  AND status_code NOT IN ('CXL', 'REJ', 'PEND')
GROUP BY instrument_id
ORDER BY total_value DESC;

query_examples.question: Show me failed trades in the last week
query_examples.query:
SELECT t.trade_id, t.instrument_id, t.counterparty_id, t.trade_date, t.settlement_date,
  t.quantity, t.net_amount, t.currency_code, t.status_code
FROM trade_ledger t
WHERE (t.status_code = 'FAIL' OR (t.status_code IN ('CONF', 'NEW') AND t.settlement_date < CURRENT_DATE()))
  AND t.trade_date >= DATEADD(day, -7, CURRENT_DATE())
ORDER BY t.settlement_date;

query_examples.question: How much commission did we pay to broker Y this quarter?
query_examples.query:
SELECT broker_id, SUM(commission) AS total_commission, COUNT(*) AS trade_count
FROM trade_ledger
WHERE broker_id = :broker_id
  AND trade_date >= DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE())
  AND status_code NOT IN ('CXL', 'REJ')
GROUP BY broker_id;

products.name: Position Book
products.description: End-of-day position snapshots showing holdings across all accounts and instruments. Contains quantity, cost basis, market value, and P&L information for portfolio management and risk reporting.
products.schema_sql:
CREATE TABLE position_book (
  position_id VARCHAR(30) PRIMARY KEY,
  instrument_id VARCHAR(20),
  account_id VARCHAR(20),
  position_date DATE,
  quantity DECIMAL(18,4),
  long_short_indicator VARCHAR(5),
  cost_basis DECIMAL(18,2),
  average_cost DECIMAL(18,6),
  market_value DECIMAL(18,2),
  unrealized_pnl DECIMAL(18,2),
  realized_pnl DECIMAL(18,2),
  total_pnl DECIMAL(18,2),
  currency_code VARCHAR(3),
  market_price DECIMAL(18,6),
  price_date DATE,
  created_date TIMESTAMP,
  last_updated_date TIMESTAMP
);

attributes.description: Unique identifier for the position record
Instrument held in this position, references instrument_master.instrument_id
Account holding this position, references account_master.account_id
Business date of this position snapshot
Number of units/shares held (positive for long, negative for short)
Whether the position is long or short
Total cost of acquiring the position
Weighted average cost per unit
Current market value of the position (quantity * market_price)
Unrealized profit/loss (market_value - cost_basis)
Realized profit/loss from closed portions of the position
Total profit/loss (unrealized + realized)
Currency of the position values
Price used for mark-to-market valuation

business_rules.description: The latest position snapshot — use the most recent position_date
Positions where we hold/own the asset
Positions where we owe the asset (short selling)
Exclude flat/zero positions which represent closed-out holdings
Positions with positive total P&L
Positions with negative total P&L

query_examples.question: What are my current positions in account X?
query_examples.query:
SELECT p.instrument_id, p.quantity, p.long_short_indicator, p.market_value, p.unrealized_pnl, p.currency_code
FROM position_book p
WHERE p.account_id = :account_id
  AND p.position_date = (SELECT MAX(position_date) FROM position_book)
  AND p.quantity != 0
ORDER BY ABS(p.market_value) DESC;

query_examples.question: Show me the top 10 positions by market value
query_examples.query:
SELECT instrument_id, account_id, quantity, market_value, unrealized_pnl, currency_code
FROM position_book
WHERE position_date = (SELECT MAX(position_date) FROM position_book)
  AND quantity != 0
ORDER BY ABS(market_value) DESC
LIMIT 10;

query_examples.question: What is the total P&L across all positions?
query_examples.query:
SELECT SUM(unrealized_pnl) AS total_unrealized,
  SUM(realized_pnl) AS total_realized,
  SUM(total_pnl) AS grand_total_pnl
FROM position_book
WHERE position_date = (SELECT MAX(position_date) FROM position_book)
  AND quantity != 0;

query_examples.question: Show me all short positions
query_examples.query:
SELECT instrument_id, account_id, quantity, market_value, unrealized_pnl, currency_code
FROM position_book
WHERE position_date = (SELECT MAX(position_date) FROM position_book)
  AND long_short_indicator = 'SHORT'
  AND quantity != 0
ORDER BY market_value ASC;

query_examples.question: What positions do we hold in instrument Y as of last Friday?
query_examples.query:
SELECT account_id, quantity, long_short_indicator, market_value, cost_basis, unrealized_pnl
FROM position_book
WHERE instrument_id = :instrument_id
  AND position_date = :as_of_date
  AND quantity != 0
ORDER BY ABS(market_value) DESC;

products.name: Fund Master
products.description: System of record for all managed funds, portfolios, and investment vehicles. Contains fund identification, classification, strategy, fee structures, AUM, NAV, and regulatory information. Covers UCITS, AIFs, 40-Act funds, ETFs, and separately managed accounts.
products.schema_sql:
CREATE TABLE fund_master (
  fund_id VARCHAR(20) PRIMARY KEY,
  fund_name VARCHAR(300),
  fund_short_name VARCHAR(100),
  fund_type_code VARCHAR(10),
  investment_strategy VARCHAR(20),
  asset_class_focus VARCHAR(10),
  benchmark_id VARCHAR(20),
  portfolio_manager_id VARCHAR(20),
  management_company_id VARCHAR(20),
  inception_date DATE,
  termination_date DATE,
  base_currency VARCHAR(3),
  nav_per_unit DECIMAL(18,6),
  total_aum DECIMAL(18,2),
  management_fee_bps DECIMAL(8,2),
  performance_fee_pct DECIMAL(8,4),
  high_water_mark DECIMAL(18,6),
  hurdle_rate DECIMAL(8,4),
  min_investment DECIMAL(18,2),
  subscription_frequency VARCHAR(10),
  redemption_frequency VARCHAR(10),
  redemption_notice_days INT,
  lockup_period_months INT,
  domicile_country VARCHAR(3),
  regulatory_framework VARCHAR(10),
  fund_status VARCHAR(5),
  created_date TIMESTAMP,
  last_updated_date TIMESTAMP
);
products.schema_graphql:
type Fund {
  fundId: ID!
  fundName: String!
  fundShortName: String
  fundTypeCode: String!
  investmentStrategy: String
  assetClassFocus: String
  benchmarkId: String
  portfolioManagerId: String
  managementCompanyId: String
  inceptionDate: Date
  terminationDate: Date
  baseCurrency: String!
  navPerUnit: Float
  totalAum: Float
  managementFeeBps: Float
  performanceFeePct: Float
  subscriptionFrequency: String
  redemptionFrequency: String
  domicileCountry: String
  regulatoryFramework: String
  fundStatus: String!
}

type Query {
  fund(fundId: ID!): Fund
  funds(filter: FundFilter, limit: Int, offset: Int): [Fund!]!
}

input FundFilter {
  fundTypeCode: String
  investmentStrategy: String
  assetClassFocus: String
  regulatoryFramework: String
  fundStatus: String
}

attributes.description: Unique identifier for the fund or portfolio
Full legal name of the fund
Abbreviated or commonly used name for the fund
Classification of the fund vehicle type
The primary investment strategy employed by the fund

value_dictionary.LONG_ONLY: Long only - traditional buy and hold

attributes.description: Primary asset class the fund invests in
Primary benchmark index against which the fund is measured, references benchmark_data.benchmark_id
Lead portfolio manager responsible for the fund
Asset management company that manages the fund, references party_master.party_id
Date when the fund was launched
Reporting and denomination currency of the fund
Net asset value per share or unit
Total assets under management in the fund's base currency
Annual management fee in basis points (e.g., 150 = 1.50%)
Performance fee as a percentage of profits (e.g., 0.20 = 20%)
Highest historical NAV used for performance fee calculation
Minimum return threshold before performance fees apply
Minimum initial investment amount
How often new subscriptions are accepted
How often redemptions are processed
Number of days notice required for redemptions
Initial lockup period in months during which redemptions are not allowed
Country where the fund is legally domiciled
Regulatory regime the fund operates under
Current operational status of the fund

value_dictionary.S: Soft-closed - closed to new investors, existing can add

business_rules.description: A fund that is currently operating and not in wind-down
business_rules.sql_predicate: fund_status IN ('A', 'S', 'H') AND termination_date IS NULL

business_rules.description: A fund that is accepting new subscriptions
Funds classified as hedge funds (typically with performance fees and lockups)
Funds with AUM greater than 1 billion in base currency
Funds compliant with the EU UCITS directive

query_examples.question: List all active equity hedge funds
query_examples.query:
SELECT fund_id, fund_name, investment_strategy, total_aum, base_currency, nav_per_unit
FROM fund_master
WHERE fund_status IN ('A', 'S', 'H')
  AND termination_date IS NULL
  AND fund_type_code = 'HEDGE'
  AND asset_class_focus = 'EQ'
ORDER BY total_aum DESC;

query_examples.question: What is the total AUM across all active funds?
query_examples.query:
SELECT SUM(total_aum) AS total_aum, base_currency, COUNT(*) AS fund_count
FROM fund_master
WHERE fund_status IN ('A', 'S', 'H')
  AND termination_date IS NULL
GROUP BY base_currency
ORDER BY total_aum DESC;

query_examples.question: Show me funds managed by portfolio manager X with their performance fees
query_examples.query:
SELECT fund_id, fund_name, fund_type_code, investment_strategy, total_aum,
  management_fee_bps, performance_fee_pct, high_water_mark, hurdle_rate
FROM fund_master
WHERE portfolio_manager_id = :pm_id
  AND fund_status IN ('A', 'S', 'H')
ORDER BY total_aum DESC;

query_examples.question: Which UCITS funds have monthly redemptions?
query_examples.query:
SELECT fund_id, fund_name, redemption_frequency, redemption_notice_days, total_aum
FROM fund_master
WHERE regulatory_framework = 'UCITS'
  AND redemption_frequency = 'MONTHLY'
  AND fund_status IN ('A', 'S', 'H')
ORDER BY fund_name;

query_examples.question: List funds with lockup periods greater than 12 months
query_examples.query:
SELECT fund_id, fund_name, fund_type_code, lockup_period_months, redemption_frequency, total_aum
FROM fund_master
WHERE lockup_period_months > 12
  AND fund_status IN ('A', 'S', 'H')
ORDER BY lockup_period_months DESC;

products.name: Benchmark Data
products.description: Index and benchmark data used for performance measurement, attribution, and mandate compliance. Includes major equity, fixed income, and multi-asset benchmarks from providers like MSCI, S&P, FTSE, and Bloomberg. Contains daily returns, constituent weights, and index levels.
products.schema_sql:
CREATE TABLE benchmark_data (
  benchmark_id VARCHAR(20),
  benchmark_name VARCHAR(200),
  benchmark_provider VARCHAR(20),
  benchmark_type VARCHAR(10),
  return_date DATE,
  index_level DECIMAL(18,6),
  daily_return DECIMAL(12,8),
  mtd_return DECIMAL(12,8),
  qtd_return DECIMAL(12,8),
  ytd_return DECIMAL(12,8),
  one_year_return DECIMAL(12,8),
  three_year_annualized DECIMAL(12,8),
  five_year_annualized DECIMAL(12,8),
  volatility_1y DECIMAL(12,8),
  sharpe_ratio_1y DECIMAL(12,8),
  constituent_count INT,
  base_currency VARCHAR(3),
  PRIMARY KEY (benchmark_id, return_date)
);

attributes.description: Unique identifier for the benchmark index
Full name of the benchmark index
Data provider or publisher of the benchmark
Asset class classification of the benchmark
Business date for the return observation
Absolute index level / value on this date
One-day total return of the index (e.g., 0.0125 = 1.25%)
Month-to-date cumulative return
Quarter-to-date cumulative return
Year-to-date cumulative return
Trailing one-year total return
Trailing three-year annualized return
Trailing five-year annualized return
Trailing one-year annualized volatility (standard deviation of returns)
Trailing one-year Sharpe ratio (excess return / volatility)
Number of securities in the index
Currency in which index returns are calculated

business_rules.description: Most recent available return date for a benchmark
business_rules.sql_predicate: return_date = (SELECT MAX(return_date) FROM benchmark_data bd2 WHERE bd2.benchmark_id = benchmark_data.benchmark_id)

business_rules.description: Equity market benchmarks only
Fixed income / bond benchmarks only

query_examples.question: What is the YTD return for the S&P 500?
query_examples.query:
SELECT benchmark_id, benchmark_name, return_date, ytd_return, one_year_return, index_level
FROM benchmark_data
WHERE benchmark_name ILIKE '%S&P 500%'
  AND return_date = (SELECT MAX(return_date) FROM benchmark_data WHERE benchmark_name ILIKE '%S&P 500%');

query_examples.question: Compare returns across all equity benchmarks MTD
query_examples.query:
SELECT benchmark_id, benchmark_name, benchmark_provider, return_date, mtd_return, ytd_return
FROM benchmark_data
WHERE benchmark_type = 'EQ'
  AND return_date = (SELECT MAX(return_date) FROM benchmark_data WHERE benchmark_type = 'EQ')
ORDER BY mtd_return DESC;

query_examples.question: Show me the 3-year and 5-year annualized returns for MSCI benchmarks
query_examples.query:
SELECT benchmark_id, benchmark_name, return_date, three_year_annualized, five_year_annualized, volatility_1y, sharpe_ratio_1y
FROM benchmark_data
WHERE benchmark_provider = 'MSCI'
  AND return_date = (SELECT MAX(return_date) FROM benchmark_data WHERE benchmark_provider = 'MSCI')
ORDER BY five_year_annualized DESC;

query_examples.question: Which benchmark had the best risk-adjusted return this year?
query_examples.query:
SELECT benchmark_id, benchmark_name, benchmark_type, ytd_return, volatility_1y, sharpe_ratio_1y
FROM benchmark_data
WHERE return_date = (SELECT MAX(return_date) FROM benchmark_data)
  AND sharpe_ratio_1y IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY sharpe_ratio_1y DESC
LIMIT 10;

products.name: Corporate Actions
products.description: Record of all corporate action events affecting instruments including dividends, stock splits, mergers, acquisitions, rights issues, spin-offs, and coupon payments. Tracks announcement, ex-date, record date, and payment date lifecycle.
products.schema_sql:
CREATE TABLE corporate_actions (
  action_id VARCHAR(30) PRIMARY KEY,
  instrument_id VARCHAR(20),
  action_type VARCHAR(15),
  announcement_date DATE,
  ex_date DATE,
  record_date DATE,
  pay_date DATE,
  effective_date DATE,
  gross_amount DECIMAL(18,6),
  net_amount DECIMAL(18,6),
  tax_rate DECIMAL(8,4),
  currency_code VARCHAR(3),
  split_ratio_from INT,
  split_ratio_to INT,
  action_status VARCHAR(10),
  description VARCHAR(500),
  created_date TIMESTAMP,
  last_updated_date TIMESTAMP
);

attributes.description: Unique identifier for the corporate action event
Instrument affected by this corporate action, references instrument_master.instrument_id
Type of corporate action event
Date the corporate action was publicly announced
Date on or after which the security trades without the entitlement
Date on which holders of record are identified for the entitlement
Date on which the cash or stock entitlement is paid/delivered
Date the corporate action takes effect (e.g., split effective date, merger completion)
Gross amount per share/unit before tax withholding
Net amount per share/unit after tax withholding
Withholding tax rate applied (e.g., 0.30 = 30%)
Currency of the payment amount
For stock splits, the original number of shares (e.g., 1 in a 1:4 split)
For stock splits, the new number of shares (e.g., 4 in a 1:4 split)
Current lifecycle status of the corporate action
Free-text description of the corporate action details

business_rules.description: Dividends that have been announced but not yet paid
business_rules.sql_predicate: action_type IN ('CASH_DIV', 'STOCK_DIV') AND action_status IN ('ANNOUNCED', 'CONFIRMED', 'EX') AND pay_date >= CURRENT_DATE()

business_rules.description: Corporate actions with future ex-dates that will affect positions
Corporate actions that have been fully processed
Cash dividend payments only
Stock split events only

query_examples.question: Show me upcoming dividend payments for my instruments
query_examples.query:
SELECT ca.action_id, ca.instrument_id, ca.action_type, ca.ex_date, ca.pay_date,
  ca.gross_amount, ca.net_amount, ca.currency_code, ca.action_status
FROM corporate_actions ca
WHERE ca.action_type IN ('CASH_DIV', 'STOCK_DIV')
  AND ca.action_status IN ('ANNOUNCED', 'CONFIRMED', 'EX')
  AND ca.pay_date >= CURRENT_DATE()
ORDER BY ca.ex_date;

query_examples.question: What dividends were paid on instrument X in the last year?
query_examples.query:
SELECT action_id, ex_date, pay_date, gross_amount, net_amount, tax_rate, currency_code
FROM corporate_actions
WHERE instrument_id = :instrument_id
  AND action_type = 'CASH_DIV'
  AND action_status = 'PAID'
  AND pay_date >= DATEADD(year, -1, CURRENT_DATE())
ORDER BY pay_date DESC;

query_examples.question: Were there any stock splits this year?
query_examples.query:
SELECT ca.action_id, ca.instrument_id, i.instrument_name,
  ca.split_ratio_from, ca.split_ratio_to, ca.effective_date, ca.action_status
FROM corporate_actions ca
JOIN instrument_master i ON ca.instrument_id = i.instrument_id
WHERE ca.action_type = 'SPLIT'
  AND ca.effective_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE())
ORDER BY ca.effective_date DESC;

query_examples.question: Show me the total dividend income by instrument this year
query_examples.query:
SELECT instrument_id, COUNT(*) AS dividend_count,
  SUM(gross_amount) AS total_gross, SUM(net_amount) AS total_net, currency_code
FROM corporate_actions
WHERE action_type = 'CASH_DIV'
  AND action_status = 'PAID'
  AND pay_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE())
GROUP BY instrument_id, currency_code
ORDER BY total_gross DESC;

query_examples.question: What corporate actions are coming up next month?
query_examples.query:
SELECT action_id, instrument_id, action_type, ex_date, pay_date, effective_date,
  gross_amount, currency_code, action_status, description
FROM corporate_actions
WHERE action_status IN ('ANNOUNCED', 'CONFIRMED')
  AND ex_date BETWEEN CURRENT_DATE() AND DATEADD(month, 1, CURRENT_DATE())
ORDER BY ex_date;

products.name: Performance Data
products.description: Fund and portfolio performance returns, attribution analysis, and benchmark comparison. Contains time-weighted and money-weighted returns at daily, monthly, and annual frequency. Includes Brinson-style performance attribution decomposition into allocation, selection, and interaction effects.
products.schema_sql:
CREATE TABLE performance_data (
  fund_id VARCHAR(20),
  return_date DATE,
  return_frequency VARCHAR(10),
  gross_return DECIMAL(12,8),
  net_return DECIMAL(12,8),
  benchmark_return DECIMAL(12,8),
  excess_return DECIMAL(12,8),
  mtd_gross_return DECIMAL(12,8),
  qtd_gross_return DECIMAL(12,8),
  ytd_gross_return DECIMAL(12,8),
  since_inception_annualized DECIMAL(12,8),
  allocation_effect DECIMAL(12,8),
  selection_effect DECIMAL(12,8),
  interaction_effect DECIMAL(12,8),
  currency_effect DECIMAL(12,8),
  total_active_return DECIMAL(12,8),
  tracking_error DECIMAL(12,8),
  information_ratio DECIMAL(12,8),
  sharpe_ratio DECIMAL(12,8),
  max_drawdown DECIMAL(12,8),
  PRIMARY KEY (fund_id, return_date, return_frequency)
);

attributes.description: Fund or portfolio for which performance is calculated, references fund_master.fund_id
Date of the performance observation
Frequency of the return observation
Period return before management and performance fees
Period return after all fees and expenses
Return of the fund's benchmark index over the same period
Fund return minus benchmark return (gross_return - benchmark_return)
Month-to-date cumulative gross return
Quarter-to-date cumulative gross return
Year-to-date cumulative gross return
Annualized return since fund inception
Performance attribution: return due to sector/asset allocation decisions vs benchmark
Performance attribution: return due to security selection within sectors
Performance attribution: combined allocation and selection interaction effect
Performance attribution: return due to currency movements
Annualized standard deviation of excess returns vs benchmark
Excess return divided by tracking error — measures risk-adjusted active return
Risk-adjusted return: (return - risk-free rate) / volatility
Maximum peak-to-trough decline over the trailing period

business_rules.description: Most recent available performance data for a fund
business_rules.sql_predicate: return_date = (SELECT MAX(return_date) FROM performance_data pd2 WHERE pd2.fund_id = performance_data.fund_id AND pd2.return_frequency = performance_data.return_frequency)

business_rules.description: When users ask for 'performance' or 'returns' without specifying frequency, default to monthly
Funds with positive excess return vs benchmark
Funds with negative excess return vs benchmark

query_examples.question: What is the YTD performance for fund X?
query_examples.query:
SELECT fund_id, return_date, ytd_gross_return, benchmark_return, excess_return, sharpe_ratio
FROM performance_data
WHERE fund_id = :fund_id
  AND return_frequency = 'MONTHLY'
  AND return_date = (SELECT MAX(return_date) FROM performance_data WHERE fund_id = :fund_id AND return_frequency = 'MONTHLY');

query_examples.question: Which funds are outperforming their benchmark this year?
query_examples.query:
SELECT p.fund_id, f.fund_name, p.ytd_gross_return, p.benchmark_return, p.excess_return, p.information_ratio
FROM performance_data p
JOIN fund_master f ON p.fund_id = f.fund_id
WHERE p.return_frequency = 'MONTHLY'
  AND p.return_date = (SELECT MAX(return_date) FROM performance_data WHERE return_frequency = 'MONTHLY')
  AND p.excess_return > 0
  AND f.fund_status IN ('A', 'S', 'H')
ORDER BY p.excess_return DESC;

query_examples.question: Show me the performance attribution breakdown for fund Y
query_examples.query:
SELECT fund_id, return_date, gross_return, benchmark_return, excess_return,
  allocation_effect, selection_effect, interaction_effect, currency_effect
FROM performance_data
WHERE fund_id = :fund_id
  AND return_frequency = 'MONTHLY'
  AND return_date >= DATEADD(month, -12, CURRENT_DATE())
ORDER BY return_date;

query_examples.question: What are the top 5 funds by Sharpe ratio?
query_examples.query:
SELECT p.fund_id, f.fund_name, p.since_inception_annualized, p.sharpe_ratio, p.max_drawdown, p.tracking_error
FROM performance_data p
JOIN fund_master f ON p.fund_id = f.fund_id
WHERE p.return_frequency = 'MONTHLY'
  AND p.return_date = (SELECT MAX(return_date) FROM performance_data WHERE return_frequency = 'MONTHLY')
  AND p.sharpe_ratio IS NOT NULL
  AND f.fund_status IN ('A', 'S', 'H')
ORDER BY p.sharpe_ratio DESC
LIMIT 5;

query_examples.question: Show monthly returns for fund Z over the last 12 months
query_examples.query:
SELECT fund_id, return_date, gross_return, net_return, benchmark_return, excess_return
FROM performance_data
WHERE fund_id = :fund_id
  AND return_frequency = 'MONTHLY'
  AND return_date >= DATEADD(month, -12, CURRENT_DATE())
ORDER BY return_date;

products.name: Risk Analytics
products.description: Risk metrics and analytics computed at fund, account, and instrument level. Includes Value at Risk (VaR), Expected Shortfall (CVaR), factor exposures, Greeks for derivatives, duration and convexity for fixed income, and portfolio-level risk decomposition. Computed daily by the risk engine.
products.schema_sql:
CREATE TABLE risk_analytics (
  risk_id VARCHAR(30) PRIMARY KEY,
  entity_type VARCHAR(10),
  entity_id VARCHAR(20),
  risk_date DATE,
  var_95_1d DECIMAL(18,2),
  var_99_1d DECIMAL(18,2),
  cvar_95_1d DECIMAL(18,2),
  var_95_10d DECIMAL(18,2),
  beta DECIMAL(12,6),
  alpha_annualized DECIMAL(12,6),
  sharpe_ratio DECIMAL(12,6),
  sortino_ratio DECIMAL(12,6),
  volatility_annualized DECIMAL(12,8),
  tracking_error DECIMAL(12,8),
  max_drawdown DECIMAL(12,8),
  duration DECIMAL(12,6),
  modified_duration DECIMAL(12,6),
  convexity DECIMAL(12,6),
  dv01 DECIMAL(18,2),
  delta DECIMAL(12,6),
  gamma DECIMAL(12,6),
  vega DECIMAL(12,6),
  theta DECIMAL(12,6),
  concentration_top10_pct DECIMAL(8,4),
  sector_concentration_pct DECIMAL(8,4),
  currency_code VARCHAR(3),
  created_date TIMESTAMP
);

attributes.description: Unique identifier for the risk record
Type of entity the risk is computed for
Identifier of the fund, account, or instrument being measured
Date the risk metrics were computed
Value at Risk at 95% confidence level over 1-day horizon — maximum expected loss
Value at Risk at 99% confidence level over 1-day horizon
Conditional VaR (Expected Shortfall) at 95% — average loss beyond VaR
Value at Risk at 95% confidence over 10-day horizon (regulatory capital)
Sensitivity of returns to benchmark/market returns
Annualized Jensen's alpha — excess return not explained by market exposure
Risk-adjusted return: (return - risk-free rate) / volatility
Downside risk-adjusted return: (return - risk-free rate) / downside deviation
Annualized volatility (standard deviation of returns)
Annualized standard deviation of excess returns vs benchmark
Maximum peak-to-trough decline
Macaulay duration for fixed income portfolios (weighted avg time to cash flows)
Modified duration — price sensitivity to a 1% change in yield
Second-order interest rate sensitivity for fixed income
Dollar value of a basis point — P&L from a 1bp yield change
Option delta — rate of change of option price with respect to underlying
Option gamma — rate of change of delta with respect to underlying
Option vega — sensitivity to a 1% change in implied volatility
Option theta — daily time decay
Percentage of portfolio held in the top 10 positions
Currency for monetary risk metrics (VaR, DV01)

business_rules.description: Most recent risk calculation date
Risk metrics aggregated at the fund level
Risk metrics for individual instruments/positions
Entities where 1-day 95% VaR exceeds a threshold (flag for review)
Portfolios where top 10 positions exceed 50% of the portfolio

query_examples.question: What is the VaR for fund X?
query_examples.query:
SELECT entity_id, risk_date, var_95_1d, var_99_1d, cvar_95_1d, var_95_10d, currency_code
FROM risk_analytics
WHERE entity_id = :fund_id
  AND entity_type = 'FUND'
  AND risk_date = (SELECT MAX(risk_date) FROM risk_analytics WHERE entity_id = :fund_id AND entity_type = 'FUND');

query_examples.question: Which funds have the highest VaR right now?
query_examples.query:
SELECT ra.entity_id, f.fund_name, ra.var_95_1d, ra.var_99_1d, ra.volatility_annualized, ra.currency_code
FROM risk_analytics ra
JOIN fund_master f ON ra.entity_id = f.fund_id
WHERE ra.entity_type = 'FUND'
  AND ra.risk_date = (SELECT MAX(risk_date) FROM risk_analytics WHERE entity_type = 'FUND')
  AND f.fund_status IN ('A', 'S', 'H')
ORDER BY ra.var_95_1d DESC
LIMIT 10;

query_examples.question: Show me the duration and DV01 for our fixed income funds
query_examples.query:
SELECT ra.entity_id, f.fund_name, ra.duration, ra.modified_duration, ra.convexity, ra.dv01, ra.currency_code
FROM risk_analytics ra
JOIN fund_master f ON ra.entity_id = f.fund_id
WHERE ra.entity_type = 'FUND'
  AND ra.risk_date = (SELECT MAX(risk_date) FROM risk_analytics WHERE entity_type = 'FUND')
  AND f.asset_class_focus = 'FI'
  AND f.fund_status IN ('A', 'S', 'H')
ORDER BY ra.dv01 DESC;

query_examples.question: What are the Greeks for our options positions?
query_examples.query:
SELECT entity_id AS instrument_id, risk_date, delta, gamma, vega, theta, currency_code
FROM risk_analytics
WHERE entity_type = 'INSTR'
  AND risk_date = (SELECT MAX(risk_date) FROM risk_analytics WHERE entity_type = 'INSTR')
  AND delta IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY ABS(delta) DESC;

query_examples.question: Which funds have the most concentrated portfolios?
query_examples.query:
SELECT ra.entity_id, f.fund_name, ra.concentration_top10_pct, ra.volatility_annualized, ra.sharpe_ratio
FROM risk_analytics ra
JOIN fund_master f ON ra.entity_id = f.fund_id
WHERE ra.entity_type = 'FUND'
  AND ra.risk_date = (SELECT MAX(risk_date) FROM risk_analytics WHERE entity_type = 'FUND')
  AND ra.concentration_top10_pct IS NOT NULL
  AND f.fund_status IN ('A', 'S', 'H')
ORDER BY ra.concentration_top10_pct DESC;

cross_product_relationships.description: Instrument issuer links to party master
Trade counterparty links to party master
Executing broker links to party master
Account owner links to party master
Account custodian links to party master
Fund's benchmark links to benchmark data for comparison
Fund management company links to party master
Performance data belongs to a fund
Fund-level risk analytics reference the fund master
Instrument-level risk analytics reference the instrument master
